meta.ogDesc: Quince leads on 37 pts after 85/104 matches

story: Lording it at the top, Quince is boring everyone into an early grave — 7 clean sheets, zero entertainment. The kind of football that makes people emigrate. Smugly in tow, Sid has turned the goalless draw into an art form nobody asked for — 6 of them. A black hole where fun goes to die. Rounding out the insufferable elite, Big Pewerk's lot defend like the door's been left on the latch, but they'll at least outscore the misery — 33 goals (GD +0), led by Netherlands (beautiful football, then a fistfight among themselves). Thrilling, doomed.

Wallowing in mid-table sludge, Ellie's defence is a public health hazard — 31 shipped, Qatar (lavishly funded, modestly threatening) leaking 10 of them. Genuinely embarrassing to be associated with. Equally forgettable, Etan will be replaying Senegal (power, pace and swagger for days)'s 5-0 all tournament because, let's be honest, it's the only good thing their team will ever do. Frame it. Barely clinging to relevance, Elsa saw something in Tunisia (stubborn, organised, impossible to love) that no scout, coach or sane human ever has — played, did nothing, and is somehow still the jewel of that abysmal squad.

Down in the dregs, Ike had to sit and watch Uzbekistan (the tournament's great unmarked mystery box) get battered 0-5 — and we'll be bringing it up at every opportunity for the rest of their natural life. And scraping the very bottom of the barrel, Murray is just... there. 23 pts, 6W 5D 10L. A complete non-entity — we genuinely forgot they were playing until just now.

The cull. 29 teams have gone home, the biggest scalp so far being Netherlands (beautiful football, then a fistfight among themselves). Sid carries the most into the knockouts with 4 of six still breathing.

Into the knockouts proper: Sid's Belgium have a date with USA; Murray's Brazil have a date with Norway; Ellie's Canada have a date with Morocco. Quince's Argentina versus Sid's Cape Verde is the pick of the all-in-the-family R32 grudge matches.
